subject: Computer Networking
title: Intrusion Detection Systems
description: A short lesson to help you apply Intrusion Detection Systems.

hook.text: A firewall blocks known threats. But what about attacks that slip through? An IDS watches for suspicious behavior.

buildup.text: An IDS monitors network traffic for patterns that match known attack signatures or unusual behavior.

discovery.text: Signature-based detection matches known attacks. Anomaly-based flags anything deviating from normal behavior.

twist.text: An IDS only alerts — it doesn't block. An IPS goes further by automatically dropping malicious traffic.

climax.text: Modern systems combine both approaches. Machine learning helps identify new attack patterns never seen before.

punchline.text: Firewalls are the lock. IDS is the alarm system.

quiz.question: What is the difference between an IDS and an IPS?
options: An IDS alerts on threats; an IPS automatically blocks them
An IDS blocks threats; an IPS only alerts
They are the same thing with different names
